section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 4: Globalisation and the Indian Economy

questions.question: What is meant by globalisation?
questions.correct_answer: Integration of countries through trade and investment
questions.explanation: Globalisation refers to the increasing interconnection between countries through trade, investment and technology.

questions.question: What are Multinational Corporations (MNCs)?
questions.correct_answer: Companies operating in more than one country
questions.explanation: MNCs set up production and offices in multiple countries to expand markets and increase profits.

questions.question: What does FDI stand for?
questions.explanation: FDI refers to investment made by an MNC in production or business in another country.

questions.question: What are trade barriers?
questions.explanation: Trade barriers such as tariffs are imposed to restrict imports and protect domestic industries.

questions.question: What major reform did India adopt in 1991?
questions.explanation: In 1991, India removed trade restrictions and opened markets to foreign investment.

questions.question: What is the main function of WTO?
questions.correct_answer: To set rules for international trade
questions.explanation: The World Trade Organization regulates and promotes fair trade among member countries.

questions.question: Name one benefit of globalisation for consumers.
questions.explanation: Globalisation provides consumers with a variety of products and better quality goods.

questions.question: How do MNCs control production globally?
questions.correct_answer: By setting up factories and partnerships abroad
questions.explanation: MNCs spread production across countries to reduce costs and increase efficiency.

questions.question: What is meant by liberalisation?
questions.explanation: Liberalisation means reducing government controls and allowing free trade and investment.

questions.question: Name one challenge of globalisation.
questions.correct_answer: Loss of business for small producers
questions.explanation: Small producers may struggle to compete with large multinational companies.

notes.title: Globalisation
notes.content: Globalisation connects countries through trade, investment, and technology, leading to economic integration.

notes.title: MNCs and FDI
notes.content: MNCs invest in other countries through FDI to expand production and markets.

notes.title: Trade Barriers
notes.content: Trade barriers such as tariffs restrict imports to protect domestic industries.

notes.title: Liberalisation 1991
notes.content: India’s 1991 reforms opened the economy to global markets and foreign investment.

notes.title: Impact of Globalisation
notes.content: Globalisation benefits consumers and certain industries but can negatively affect small producers.

facts.fact: India became a member of the WTO in 1995.
Reduction in trade barriers increases competition in domestic markets.
The IT sector in India has grown significantly due to globalisation.
MNCs often locate production where labour is cheaper.
Consumers benefit from better quality and lower prices due to competition.
